A_protocol_models.description: Protocol-level message models for tool calls (user-facing, discriminator-keyed). These are the shapes that flow through the LLM conversation.

ToolCall.fields: state: 'call', step?: NonNegativeInt, toolCallId: string, toolName: string, args: unknown
ToolCall.note: Sent by the LLM to request a tool execution. State='call'.

ToolPartialCall.fields: state: 'partial-call', step?: NonNegativeInt, toolCallId: string, toolName: string, args: unknown
ToolPartialCall.note: Streaming partial — args are built up incrementally as the LLM streams.

ToolResult.fields: state: 'result', step?: NonNegativeInt, toolCallId: string, toolName: string, args: unknown, result: string
ToolResult.note: Sent back to the LLM after tool execution completes.

ToolInvocation.fields: Union of ToolCall | ToolPartialCall | ToolResult, discriminated by 'state'
ToolInvocation.note: Unified discriminated union for all tool message types.

B_tool_part_state_machine.description: Database-level ToolPart states. These are the persistent states stored in PartTable. Each state carries different fields. This IS the state machine — the status field is the discriminator.

pending.fields: status: 'pending', input: Record<string, any>, raw: string
pending.description: Tool call has been received from the LLM but execution has not yet started. Created by ensureToolCall().

running.fields: status: 'running', input: Record<string, any>, time: {start}, title?: string, metadata?: Record
running.description: Tool is actively executing. Updated by SessionTools.context().metadata() callback.

completed.fields: status: 'completed', input: Record, output: string, time: {start, end, compacted?}, attachments?: FilePart[]
completed.description: Tool execution succeeded. Set by finishToolCall() with status: 'completed'.

error.fields: status: 'error', input: Record, error: string, time: {start, end}
error.description: Tool execution failed (any error: schema validation, timeout, runtime error). Set by finishToolCall() with status: 'error'.

ToolPart.fields: {type: 'tool', callID: string, tool: string, state: ToolState, metadata?: Record} plus base fields (id, sessionID, messageID)
ToolPart.note: The complete part stored in the database.

transition_table: pending → running (when context().metadata() is called, IF current status is 'pending' or 'running')
pending → error (if finishToolCall errors before metadata callback fires)

C_runtime_tool_call_lifecycle.description: In-memory lifecycle managed by SessionProcessor. Uses Deferred to signal completion.

ToolCall.fields: {partID: string, messageID: string, sessionID: string, done: Deferred<void>, inputEnded: boolean}
ToolCall.description: Internal runtime tracking for each tool call. Stored in ctx.toolcalls record.

ensureToolCall.behavior: If toolCallID doesn't exist in ctx.toolcalls: create a ToolPart with ToolStatePending in the database + create a ToolCall entry with a new Deferred. If exists: return existing. This is idempotent — called for both partial-call streams and final tool-call events.

readToolCall.behavior: Looks up ctx.toolcalls[toolCallID]. Errors if not found.

updateToolCall.behavior: Reads the tool call, applies the update function to the ToolPart, persists it. The update function is provided by callers (e.g., metadata callback changes pending→running).

finishToolCall.signature: (toolCallID, result: {status: 'completed'|'error', output?: string, error?: Error}) => Effect<void>
finishToolCall.behavior: Updates ToolPart state to completed or error, persists, then calls settleToolCall() to resolve the Deferred.

settleToolCall.behavior: Calls Deferred.succeed(undefined) on ctx.toolcalls[toolCallID].done and removes from ctx.toolcalls. This unblocks any awaiter of the Deferred.

completeToolCall.behavior: Backward-compat wrapper. Calls finishToolCall with status: 'completed'.

D_tool_definition_and_execution.description: Canonical tool definition API. Tools are defined with Effect-based init and execute.

Tool.Def.fields: {id, description, parameters, execute, jsonSchema?, formatValidationError?, cacheable?, timeout?, retry?, maxConcurrency?, errorTaxonomy?}

Tool.Context.fields: {sessionID, messageID, agent, abort, callID?, extra?, messages, metadata(input), ask(input)}

Tool.ExecuteResult.fields: {title: string, metadata: M, output: string, attachments?: FilePart[]}

Tool.define.description: Returns an Info object with id and lazy init(). The init() returns a Def with wrapped execute.

Tool.wrap.description: Internal — wraps execute in pipeline: decode args → execute → truncate output → [retry] → [timeout] → [semaphore] → span attributes. Uses Effect.orDie at the end — errors surface as defects.

Tool.init.description: Resolves a tool Info into a concrete Def.

E_tool_result_types.description: Typed, structured tool results — wraps raw execution output with status, failures, suggested next actions.

ToolStatus.description: The 4 possible outcomes for every tool execution.

ToolFailure.fields: {test?: string, file?: string, line?: number, message: string}

NextAffordance.fields: {action: string, tool: string, description: string, priority?: string}

TypedToolResult.fields: {status: ToolStatus, tool: string, summary: string, data?: T, output?: string, failures?: ToolFailure[], nextAffordances?: NextAffordance[]}

wrapExecute.description: Wraps raw tool execution in TypedResult. All tool results carry typed failure info + suggested next actions, even on defects.

classifyError.description: Classifies unknown errors into errorKind + recoverable flag by pattern-matching _tag strings.

formatOutput.description: Serializes TypedToolResult to string for LLM consumption. Appends structured marker for parseOutput.

parseOutput.description: Parses a formatted output string back to TypedToolResult.

checkPhaseGate.description: Checks if the tool is allowed by the current lifecycle phase gate.

F_execution_events.description: Bus events published during tool execution for coordination/observability.

G_session_tool_resolution.description: Bridges Tool.Def → AI SDK Tool. Resolves tools per-agent, builds execute callbacks with permission checks and state updates.

resolve.signature: (input: {agent, model, session, processor, bypassAgentCheck, messages, promptOps}) => Effect<Record<string, AITool>>
behavior: 1. Gets tools from ToolRegistry.Service.tools() filtered by agent
2. For built-in tools: wraps execute in TypedResult.wrapExecute → updates ToolPart state to running (via ctx.metadata callback)
3. For MCP tools: wraps execute with permission ask → updates ToolPart state
4. Uses readSemaphore(10) for cacheable tools and writeSemaphore(3) for mutating tools

tool_context_creation.note: Includes metadata callback that updates ToolPart state from pending to running (only if current status is 'pending' or 'running')

H_registry.description: Central tool registry — resolves built-in, plugin, and MCP tools. Returns Tool.Def objects.

I_campaign_integration.description: Campaign layer defines tool limits per role.

key_contracts.maxToolCalls: Per-role limit on total tool calls (varies 60-200 per role). Defined in role contracts. Not a runtime guard — informational limit for campaign orchestration.

J_where_tool_calls_can_get_stuck.description: Identified failure modes in the tool call lifecycle.

stuck_scenarios.title: Pending without executor
stuck_scenarios.condition: ToolPart is created with state=pending but the execute callback never fires (or the tool resolution fails, or the LLM stream drops the tool call after partial-call).
stuck_scenarios.recovery: No automatic timeout on the pending state. The Deferred in processor ToolCall never resolves. The session loop continues but the tool call is orphaned.

stuck_scenarios.title: Schema validation failure → orDie
stuck_scenarios.condition: Tool.wrap() uses Effect.orDie at line 247. If Schema.decodeUnknownEffect fails (InvalidArgumentsError), it becomes a defect, not a typed error. The TypedResult.wrapExecute catches it, but if wrapExecute is bypassed, the defect kills the fiber.

stuck_scenarios.title: Timeout without TypedResult
stuck_scenarios.condition: Tool.timeout is configured but the wrap pipeline doesn't properly convert TimeoutError. The Effect.timeoutOption catches it, but the orDie means it's a defect upstream. If the caller doesn't use TypedResult.wrapExecute, the timeout becomes an unhandled defect.

stuck_scenarios.title: MCP tool execute() is undefined
stuck_scenarios.condition: In tools.ts:144, MCP tools with undefined execute are silently skipped with 'continue'. No error is surfaced to the caller.

stuck_scenarios.title: Phase gate blocks silently
stuck_scenarios.condition: PhaseGate.checkAllowed fails but the error might not propagate correctly. checkPhaseGate in typed-result.ts returns Option<TypedToolResult> — the caller in tools.ts line 99 uses yield* but may not handle the returned option correctly.

stuck_scenarios.title: Double metadata call overwrites state
stuck_scenarios.condition: In tools.ts line 58, the metadata callback checks `if (!['running', 'pending'].includes(match.state.status)) return match`. If something else changed the state between pending and the metadata callback, the transition to running silently fails and the tool stays stuck in pending.

K_processor_stream_loop.description: The main process() function at line 808. Streams LLM events and dispatches tool calls.
behavior: 1. Calls LLM.Service.stream() with the resolved tools map
2. Processes events from the stream: text-delta, tool-call, tool-result, finish, error
3. On 'tool-call' event: calls ensureToolCall to create pending ToolPart + Deferred
4. The AI SDK internally calls the tool's execute() callback (defined in session/tools.ts resolve)
5. execute() calls TypedResult.wrapExecute → result goes back to session/tools.ts
6. Session/tools.ts calls processor.updateToolCall (pending→running) and then processor.completeToolCall (running→completed/error)
7. On 'finish' event: checks if any tool calls are still pending (ctx.toolcalls still has entries). If so, waits for them via Deferred.await
8. On 'error' event: maps errors to MessageV2 error types

K_machine_interaction.description: Agent machine definitions declare lifecycle phases that intersect with tool access.
machine_ids: general-man, secretary, cartographer, surveyor, compass, soundings, logbook
architect, foundation, load-bearer, building-inspector, blueprint, zoning-board
critic, witness, coroner, precedent, blast-radius, reasonable-doubt, exhibit-a, appeal
surgeon, scalpel, vitals, stress-test, second-opinion, tourniquet, monitor
trial, journalist, scoop, editor, byline, press, retort, headline
K_machine_interaction.note: Machine definitions use deps (git, rg, fd, log, spawn, sendDirective). Task/delegate tools spawn subagents. Runtime communicates via coordination claims, not direct tool calls.

L_acp_events.description: ACP protocol events for tool call lifecycle (for IDE/cockpit consumers).

toolCallUpdate.note: ToolCallUpdate with content for completed/error tools

toolCallUpdateRunning.note: ACP update when tool transitions to running

M_campaign_event_system.description: State machine tracking lane progress through tool events.
M_campaign_event_system.note: These events track lane progress. Tool calls themselves are NOT campaign events — the campaign observes tool call results through predicates.

orphan_deltas.description: tool-input-delta without preceding tool-input-start silently dropped per comment: 'Match dev: silently drop orphan deltas'

provider_executed_tools.description: Some providers execute tools server-side (detected via metadata.providerExecuted). Processor skips local execution entirely.

abandoned_toolcalls_on_termination.description: On stream termination, cleanup() iterates remaining toolcalls, awaits Deferred with 250ms timeout, marks unfinishable ones as error.

llm_repair.description: experimental_repairToolCall — called by AI SDK when tool input fails validation. Attempts lowercase name match then retry.

K_tests.description: Existing tests for tool call infrastructure.

test_files.covers: Tool.define() — object mutation, effect-defined freshness, parameter decoding, InvalidArgumentsError
Tool registry failure tools (inspect_failure, report) resolve correctly and summarize failed calls
Snapshot consistency during tool execution. Uses SessionProcessor.layer and tests tool_call race conditions.

coverage_gaps: No test for ToolStatePending → ToolStateRunning → ToolStateCompleted full lifecycle end-to-end
No test for Deferred lifecycle (pending indefinitely, cleanup timeout)
No test for finishToolCall with both completed and error paths
No test for the metadata callback state guard (STUCK-6)
No test for MCP tool with undefined execute (STUCK-4)
No test for TypedResult.wrapExecute error classification
No test for execution.ts bus events (publishStarted/Completed/Failed)
No test for 'timed_out' at execution level (only listed as terminal state)
No test for 'awaiting_approval' non-terminal state through permission-ask path
No test for 'schema_error' at ToolState level through LLM-generated invalid args
No test for 'permission_denied' at ToolStateError level
No test for TransientError retry loop recovery
No test for ToolError recoverable flag behavior divergence
No test for abort signal reaching tool during execution (only during streaming)
No test for 'queued' non-terminal state (declared but never exercised)

key_test_files_detailed.description: Pure TS invariants: defines TERMINAL_STATES (completed, failed, cancelled, timed_out, permission_denied, schema_error, execution_error) and NON_TERMINAL_STATES (pending, running, awaiting_approval, queued). Tests mutual exclusivity, schema failure→error transitions, empty-text loop breaker, runaway detection, actor identity stability, and max duration invariant.
key_test_files_detailed.note: References upstream issues #30093 (stuck pending tool calls) and #28507 (infinite empty loops)

key_test_files_detailed.description: Integration: AI SDK tool call completion, pending tool abort on cleanup, aborted error recording, interruption abort without manual abort. Uses TestLLMServer mock.

Heavy integration: session loop, tool call loop continuation, orphan tool calls, abort/interrupt/cancel, snapshot race with tool execution, tool metadata preservation, empty assistant turn loop breaker. ~1700 lines of test code.
Reproduces race condition: AI SDK executes tools internally before snapshot system captures state.

tool_call_lifecycle_summary.entry: ensureToolCall() in processor.ts:242 — creates pending ToolPart + Deferred

transitions.guard: Only if current status is 'pending' or 'running'

transitions.trigger: finishToolCall with error before metadata callback fires

termination: settleToolCall() resolves Deferred<void>, removes from ctx.toolcalls
completed/error are terminal ToolState values (no further state transitions)

parallel_tracking.database: ToolPart rows in PartTable via MessageV2
parallel_tracking.runtime: ctx.toolcalls record via processor ToolCall + Deferred
